brand.name: DLens
brand.tagline: Collected posts become the right kind of working object.
brand.description: A grill-me synthesis deck for the three DLens intelligence modes.

slides.subtitle: Product decisions, topic case notes, and PR campaign evidence should not share the same card grammar.
slides.lead: The same Threads collection shell can feed three sharply different workflows. Each mode has a different buyer, primary object, cost timing, and export promise.
slides.meta: Grill-me synthesis · Product / Topic / PR Evidence · May 2026

slides.lead: DLens should not pretend every saved post wants the same analysis. The mode decides what the post becomes.

slides.body: The extension should keep one reliable collection surface: hover, preview, save, visible metrics, source URL. After save, each workspace routes the post into a different object.
bullets: Product mode saves a signal for product relevance and batch agent brief output.
Topic mode saves source material for topic notes, clusters, and pair lens evidence.
PR Evidence mode saves evidence rows for campaign criteria matching and CSV export.
Archive remains a quiet no-AI storage mode, not a fourth intelligence promise.

slides.lead: It asks whether a collected discussion contains a reusable product pattern, workflow, demand, technical reference, competitor cue, or noise.

slides.body: Product mode opens directly into classification. AI pre-labels a primary category and supporting tags. The saved area is a signal pool, not a Kanban board.
bullets: Primary categories: All / Try / Demand / Technical workflow / Marketing reference / Competitor / Learning / Noise / Insufficient data.
Card hierarchy: reusable insight headline, concrete technical reference, why it matters to the user's product, one representative quote, then evidence detail.
Cost boundary: heavy work happens on batch export or explicit single-card prompt generation, not by default on every save.
Main CTA: copy selected saved signals as an Agent Brief for the user's own downstream agent.

slides.body: Product mode earns trust when the first line is reusable and actionable: a pattern the user can paste into notes, a brief, or an agent task.
bullets: Not: This post discusses recurring crawlers.
Yes: Wrap recurring crawl into a daily monitoring agent before building a full dashboard.
Use original text first; keep output medium-length; preserve enough source material for the next agent.
Single-card prompt is optional and explicit. Batch Agent Brief is the default cost-conscious action.

slides.lead: It treats collected discussions like research material: topic first, clusters second, pair lens only inside a topic detail.

slides.body: Topic mode should surface topics first, not isolated posts. It is for PR, journalists, analysts, researchers, or founders tracking how groups react to a situation.
bullets: Main output: Topic Brief / Case Note.
Primary object: Topic Note. Secondary object: Cluster Card.
Cluster labels use two layers: formal reaction type plus readable nickname.
Cluster UI can show list plus bubble preview; clicking a cluster returns the user to evidence.
Pair Lens is downgraded into Topic Detail: select two source posts via Add to compare, then save a short Pair Insight into Topic Note.

slides.body: Topic mode can feel more editorial and research-oriented, but it should not become a feature museum. It needs enough structure to compare social reactions without pretending every thread has deep linguistic signal.
bullets: Useful sections: topic overview, cluster map, evidence list, source list, saved notes.
The wording / tone layer is optional when threads are too fragmented.
The core expansion should be cluster visualization and evidence drill-back.
Library should support Topic Note plus Cluster Card as saved knowledge objects.

slides.lead: It does not sell social listening. It helps PR operators turn already-found posts into a defendable campaign evidence CSV and topline audit summary.

slides.body: V1 is for agency operators who already found the posts. DLens reduces copying, classification, and report preparation before the data lands in Excel or Google Sheets.
bullets: Campaign setup: name, brief / guideline, AI-suggested six criteria, user-editable labels.
Collect: save opened Threads posts into current campaign rows with visible metrics.
Batch match: one explicit action fills six criteria columns with ✓ / blank.
UI: compact ledger shows author, caption snippet, key metrics, matched count, collected time.
Export: CSV is primary; topline PR audit summary is secondary and facts-first.

stats.label: PR rows
stats.sub: Batch matched, not one call per row

stats.label: Collector
stats.sub: Shared capture shell, separate output contracts

slides.caption: Archive remains no-AI storage. It is a utility posture, not a primary intelligence mode.

quote.text: is when the user feels DLens saved a judgment they used to spend two hours defending.

slides.body: Next: prototype this visual direction as a design artifact first. Then implement only the architecture that survives review: mode routing, PR campaign storage, Product batch export, Topic detail Pair Lens.

slides.footer: Product intelligence · Topic casebook · PR evidence · MMXXVI
